metadata.title: Why Individual Caution Does Not Add Up to Team Safety

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Namaste, Liam — in for Bear. No one knew? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.segment: Why Individual Caution Does Not Add

beats.narration_text: Five individually careful people are using agents on a team with no shared rules. One adds an MCP server to read the shared Dropbox. The server is added at the account level. All five members' agents now have read access to every file in Dropbox — including client contracts marked confidential. No one intended this. No one knew.
beats.scene_description: a shared workspace with multiple team members at laptops — editorial collage, newsprint texture, no faces

beats.narration_text: Five individually careful people using agents should produce careful team-level agent use. Here is the case where five careful individuals produced no shared protection. Why?
beats.scene_description: question card — CARD copy: Why does individual caution not add up to team safety?

beats.narration_text: Individual practice does not aggregate into team safety when agents act on shared assets. A connector added for one person's use may be visible to the entire team account. Accountability gaps emerge precisely at the boundaries between individuals — not inside each person's careful practice, but in the shared space between them.
beats.scene_description: five cautious individual nodes inside small TEAL fences — all connected to a single shared folder in the center with no fence around it. CRIMSON highlight on the shared center.
beats.new_visual_element: [MANIM] five fenced nodes plus unprotected shared center

beats.narration_text: The gap is in the shared space — not in any individual's practice.
beats.scene_description: mechanism card — CARD copy: The gap is in the shared space, not in individual practice.

beats.narration_text: A four-person research team each uses Claude individually and carefully. One member adds an MCP server to read the shared Dropbox. The server is added at the account level — not just for that person. All four members' agents now have read access to every file in Dropbox, including client contracts marked confidential. No one intended this. No one knew.
beats.scene_description: four individual nodes — one connects MCP server. Arrow shows account-level addition. All four nodes gain access to shared Dropbox. Confidential contract file in CRIMSON.

beats.narration_text: The fix requires shared rules before individual use begins. Name which data each agent can reach. Define who must approve before a connector is added at the account level. Agree on what leaves the team account and who is accountable when a client report contains an error. These are not policies — they are the fence around the shared space.
beats.scene_description: left (CRIMSON): five nodes each with individual fences, shared center unprotected. Right (TEAL): shared center gains its own fence — labeled with three rules: data scope, connector approval, accountability.
beats.new_visual_element: [MANIM] unprotected shared vs fenced shared center

beats.narration_text: Before any team member adds an agent capability: ask what shared systems it touches. Ask whether it is added for one account or for all. Name who owns the decision. Write it down before anyone connects anything.
beats.scene_description: three TEAL checklist chips: 'What shared systems does it touch?' / 'One account or all?' / 'Who owns the decision?' Each chip appears in sequence.

beats.narration_text: Five careful individuals do not automatically produce a careful team. The shared space needs its own fence.
beats.scene_description: endcard — CARD copy: Five careful individuals do not make a careful team. The shared space needs its own fence. / sub: AGENTIC AI

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Before any team member adds an agent capability: ask what shared systems it touches. Ask whether it is added for one account or for all. Name who owns the decision. Write it down before anyone connects anything. Five careful individuals do not automatically produce a careful team. The shared space needs its own fence.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why Individual Caution Does Not Add Up to Team Safety] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
